document.title: Living Folders Orientation
document.purpose: Orient humans and LLMs to the control-panel and directory-map form of Living Folders.

keystone: A directory should be allowed to present itself according to its purpose.
The folder remains the authority; Living Folders is a semantic and operational lens.

navigation: invalid paths restore the actual current path
back, parent, refresh, and choose-folder controls
permanent Explorer and shell escape hatches
presentation: a folder presentation is inferred when no override exists
the Presentation combobox may save an explicit override
Auto removes the override and returns authority to inference
Directory Map is a presentation alongside Project Root, Workbench, Factory, Library, Gallery, Inbox, Vault, Warehouse, Hallway, and Ruin
Temporal is a general chronological interpretation; Daystream is its first layout
folder-buttons: declared navigation and command buttons are stored in .living-folder/description.json
navigation buttons do not require code trust
command buttons and detected runnable files remain disabled until the user explicitly trusts local code
the button editor adds, edits, and removes navigation or command buttons
small up and down arrow controls, or Control plus Up and Control plus Down, reorder declared and detected buttons together
directory-map: immediate directory contents appear as movable and resizable rectangles
folders are yellow, executables red, text blue, JSON light blue, images purple, and other files gray
double-click follows folders or asks the operating system to open files
committed geometry is stored by entry name in the folder constitution
double-clicking empty map space creates persistent styled text
map text supports left, center, or right alignment; three font sizes; white, green, blue, or red color; dragging; and double-click re-editing
editing existing map text exposes Delete before Cancel and Save; newly created text does not
map text may be a Labelled Region: a resizable empty outlined box whose label interrupts the top border according to its alignment
map-images: the Add Image button, native file drop, and Ctrl+V clipboard paste import images
original image bytes are stored by SHA-256 identity under .living-folder/images
map image position, width, and height live in description.json
map images can be dragged and resized using their lower-right handle
double-clicking a map image opens its stored original through the operating system
Page Up moves the selected file, folder, text, or image one layer toward the front
Page Down moves the selected element one layer toward the back
dragging from empty canvas creates a marquee whose overlapping visible elements glow during the gesture
marquee release commits a mixed group selection across folders, files, text, labelled regions, and images
dragging any member of a group selection moves the whole group without enabling group resize
clicking one item replaces the group with that single selection; clicking empty canvas clears all selection
new immediate filesystem entries wait in a fixed Incoming dock rather than being auto-placed
clicking an Incoming chip enters placement mode; clicking the map commits one semantic placement event
an Incoming chip can also be dragged across canvases; only its map-side preview is animated and release commits placement
Incoming context actions are Place, Open in System, Ignore or Hide, and Open Shell Here only for child folders
ignored entries can be returned to Incoming through the Ignored dialog
placed entries missing from disk remain as dim red wireframe ghosts
Delete on a missing ghost removes its retained map record without filesystem confirmation
temporal-folder: Temporal inference activates when at least three immediate entries contain valid YYYY-MM-DD dates and at least half of eligible entries participate
an entry named exactly YYYY-MM-DD is canonical; a dated filename is associated with the same day node
Daystream projects continuous Monday-first weeks rather than paged calendar months
the initial position is the latest occupied node with one following week of context by default
the temporal minimap summarizes activity by month and jumps the main surface through time
folder navigation and command buttons remain available immediately below the global Presentation strip and before the Daystream title
unmatched entries remain visible in the bottom exceptions shelf; backup names matching *~ are hidden by default
single-click selects a day; double-click smart-opens one canonical entry or opens a temporal cockpit for ambiguity
double-clicking an empty day offers to create its canonical YYYY-MM-DD folder
Shift plus double-click creates the canonical day folder directly
Alt plus double-click reveals matching entries; right-click opens the temporal context menu
wheel moves one week, Shift plus wheel approximately one month, and Control plus wheel one year
arrow keys move the selected day; Enter opens it; Space opens its cockpit; N creates it; T/Home jumps to today; M/End jumps to the latest occupied day; R opens the raw control-panel view
Temporal interpretation never moves, renames, or rewrites the entries it gathers
storage: the first mutation of a legacy folder writes the current directory form while leaving the legacy file untouched

architecture.cli: lionscliapp owns declaration, dispatch, persistent app configuration, execpath.open-at coercion, and Tk single-instance runtime behavior.
architecture.world-model: The filesystem plus .living-folder/description.json and its content-addressed image tree own durable folder reality. Temporal nodes are derived projections over immediate entries.
architecture.discrete-engine: Semantic events reduce into compact navigation, trust, selection, and committed map state, producing explicit effects.
architecture.continuity: Tk pointer callbacks own in-progress map move and resize previews. Only release emits committed semantic meaning.
architecture.projection: Tk widgets and canvas items are downstream renderings and do not decide semantic truth.
architecture.effects: Folder reads, atomic JSON writes, process launches, and OS opening occur outside the reducer.

runtime-model.stable-execroot: Living Folders should be launched with an explicit stable --execroot such as C:\lion\runtime\living-folders.
runtime-model.instance-owner: lionscliapp's Tk runtime owns instance.json and inbox/ under the resolved .living-folders-cli project directory.
runtime-model.summons: A second invocation of the single-instance Tk command writes a summon message into inbox/ and exits. The resident application polls through lionscliapp's Tk attachment and responds on the Tk event loop.
launchers: launchers\living-folders.bat uses pythonw and passes --execroot plus optional --execpath.open-at.
launchers\living-folders-debug.bat uses python and passes the same arguments for console-visible debugging.
runtime-model.special-note: Living Folders no longer depends on Machine Root or a custom auxiliary summon runtime to obtain machine-stable single-instance behavior.
runtime-model.packaging-note: The package no longer installs a bare living-folders script entry. The expected launch paths are python -m livingfolders ... or the provided batch launchers.
